MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS
Off Calendar Feb-27-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. JENNIFER BROWN, Esq. of Brown, Pennington (Tel: 415-864-2727) for Defendant Floyd Trammell.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT JENNIFER BROWN, FLOYD TRAMMELL, ESTATE OF GENETTE TRAMMELL's MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Per CCP 128(a)(8), the court stays this action. The court previously determined that plaintiff pleads a claim based on defendants' alleged agreement to advance costs (reporter fees). In any event, the court believes that the Probate Court may be able to resolve the fee dispute or at least circumscribe the issues that this court would need to rule upon. Plaintiff should make an application to the Probate Court regarding this fee dispute. If the defendants hamper plaintiff's efforts to obtain a fee award in the Probate Court, the Probate Court determines that it cannot accord plaintiff relief or if some other compelling circumstance warrants it, plaintiff may apply to lift the stay.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-03-2023 Set For Trial On May-20-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-08-2023 Continued To Nov-15-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-17-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Jose A. Cordova, Esq., The Law Office of Jose A. Cordova, 415-466-8211,
jac@cordova-lawfirm.com, Pro Per Plaintiff.
Jennifer L. Brown, Esq., Brown, Pennington, 415-864-6767, JLBrown@Brown-Pennington.com, for Defendant Floyd Trammell, as an Individual and as Executor to the Estate of Genette Trammell

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JENNIFER BROWN, FLOYD TRAMMELL's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendants' demurrer is overruled.

Defendants argue that the demurrer should be sustained because the complaint was filed in the wrong department, plaintiff fails to state a cause of action because no attorney's fees have been granted or ordered to be paid by the probate court, and the complaint defects are incurable.

Plaintiff argues that defendants agreed to seek the attorneys' fees in probate court but have not done so in over two years. (Opp., 5:8-9.) Plaintiff also argues that defendant Trammell agreed to advance out of pocket expenses. (See Cmplnt. at p. 3.) Each cause of action refers to the $3,500 in costs for court reporter fees, that is still outstanding, in addition to the unpaid attorney's fees.

First, the matter was properly filed in the civil law and motion department because it involves contractual disputes between the parties.

Plaintiff's allegations in the complaint are sufficiently alleged at the pleading stage. Plaintiff alleges that there was an agreement that the out-of-pocket expenses would be advanced, they have not, and the court reporter is now demanding payment from plaintiff. Defendants' demurrer makes no mention of the agreement to advance costs or the court reporter fees. The court need not make any finding with regard to the attorneys' fees because the allegations regarding the court reporter fees are properly pleaded. (Elder v. Pacific Bell Telephone Co. (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 365, 385 ["general demurrer does not lie as to a portion of a cause of action and, if any part of a cause of action is properly pleaded, the demurrer will be overruled."].)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-25-2023 Continued To Aug-17-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
